title: Local review preserves moderator-owned suggested-code rendering and positive-feedback semantics through the presentation layer

provenance.originSummary: Synthetic benchmark scenario designed to exercise the rule directly.
provenance.whyItMattered: Use this case to benchmark the new moderator-owned presentation-hint model, render-only language-tagged suggested-code output contract, and positive-feedback priority semantics through the generic local review prompt.
provenance.genericCondition: Sanitized fixture models the new moderator-plus-presentation flow where surviving findings carry deterministic presentation hints and the final renderer must preserve Current Code and Suggested Code blocks without prompt-side derivation while also keeping `good` and `notable` positive-feedback strengths semantically distinct.

description: A sanitized local-review scenario where the moderator, handoff schema, and presentation contract all change together. The final rendered review should preserve structured finding cards with language-tagged suggested-code blocks driven by moderator-owned presentation hints rather than prompt-side invention, and should keep positive-feedback display levels distinct between baseline `good` work and above-and-beyond `notable` work.

scope.notes: Sanitized fixture models the new moderator-plus-presentation flow where surviving findings carry deterministic presentation hints and the final renderer must preserve Current Code and Suggested Code blocks without prompt-side derivation while also keeping `good` and `notable` positive-feedback strengths semantically distinct.

mustCatch.description: The review should raise a real issue if wording lets the prompt invent rich-display semantics instead of requiring surviving moderated findings to carry the canonical `presentation` hints.

The final rendered review should preserve a structured finding card that includes `Current Code`, `Suggested Code`, and a fenced code block using the source language when the moderated record carries those hints.

The final rendered review should preserve positive-feedback structured findings so ordinary standards-compliant work renders as `✅ Good` while above-and-beyond non-obvious implementation work renders as `⭐ Notable`.

mustNotFlag.description: The workflow must not accept prompt-side derivation of review type, suggested change, current code, corrected code, or code language once moderator-owned presentation hints exist.

notes: Use this case to benchmark the new moderator-owned presentation-hint model, render-only language-tagged suggested-code output contract, and positive-feedback priority semantics through the generic local review prompt.
